Cebadilla (Plantago psyllium), also known as Cebatha villosa, is a plant species that belongs to the Plantaginaceae family, commonly known as the plantain family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including treating digestive issues, lowering cholesterol levels, and aiding in the management of diabetes due to its ability to help regulate blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Cebadilla include mucilages, polysaccharides, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cebadilla include capsules, tablets, and powders, often used to support digestive health and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
